title: GitHub API response shapes consumed by the spec detail view
description: Documents only the fields actually used by Vantage when rendering a spec detail page. Source: GitHub REST API v3.

FileContent.description: Response shape from GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/contents/{path} when path is a file. Used to retrieve the spec markdown content.

type.description: Must be 'file'; if 'dir' the path resolves to a directory and Vantage returns a not-found state.

path.description: Full path within the repo, e.g. 'openspec/changes/spec-detail-view/specs/feature-spec-detail-view.md'.

content.description: Base64-encoded file content. Vantage decodes this with Buffer.from(content, 'base64').toString('utf-8').

encoding.description: Always 'base64' for file blobs from the GitHub API.

DirectoryListing.description: Response shape from GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/contents/{path} when path is a directory. Used to list contracts/ and tests/ files.

type.description: Vantage only surfaces entries with type 'file'.

CommitSummary.description: First element of the response from GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/commits?path={specPath}&per_page=1. Used to extract last committer identity and date.

name.description: Git author name (display name from the commit metadata).

date.description: ISO 8601 commit date. Vantage formats this as 'Updated [Month] [Day], [Year]'.

author.description: GitHub user object for the committer. May be null if the git email is not associated with a GitHub account.

login.description: GitHub username, e.g. 'koeneveraert'. Displayed in the title section.

avatar_url.description: URL of the GitHub user's avatar image. Displayed alongside the username in the title section.
